🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: Crypto's bear market deepens — CZ blames AI rotation, US-Iran tensions and the four-year cycle. The strain is showing at the top: Strategy's premium evaporates and Binance loses the EU, even as Tether and Japan build new on-chain rails. ⚖️ Binance Locked Out of the EU as MiCA Deadline Hits Binance told users in Poland, Italy, Spain and France it will suspend services from July 1 after failing to secure a MiCA license, having withdrawn its Greek application amid reported concerns over money-laundering controls and whether CZ passes the "fit and proper" test. It plans to reapply through France, with user assets staying accessible during the wind-down. Read full story 📉 Strategy's Market Value Slips Below Its Own Bitcoin For the first time, Strategy's enterprise mNAV fell below 1 — the market now values the whole company (about $50.4B) at less than the bitcoin it holds (around $51.1B), inverting the premium that powered its buy-everything model. Issuing new shares is now dilutive, casting doubt over one of bitcoin's biggest sources of incremental demand. More details 🛡️ DOJ Seizes Huione's Crypto-Laundering Backbone The Justice Department seized a cloud account hosting backend infrastructure for Cambodia's Huione Group, which it branded one of the world's most prolific criminal marketplaces — tied to laundering billions from investment fraud, cyber scams and North Korean heists. FinCEN moved the same day to extend its ban to a successor entity to stop the group rebranding around restrictions. Read more 🥇 Tether Puts Its $23B Gold Pile to Work Tether is bringing tokenized gold (XAUT) to crypto lender Ledn, with bullion-backed loans due later this year that let holders borrow against gold rather than sell it. Backed by roughly $23 billion — about 140 tons of bullion in Swiss vaults — the move deepens Tether's pivot from stablecoin issuer to diversified finance group. See details 🇯🇵 Japan Debuts Its First Trust-Bank-Backed Yen Stablecoin SBI launched JPYSC — issued by SBI Shinsei Trust Bank and distributed via SBI VC Trade — Japan's first trust-backed, FSA-approved yen stablecoin, with roughly $70 million minted on day one. Crucially, it carries no transaction cap, opening the door to institutional-scale settlement and tokenized real-world assets that earlier capped yen tokens could not support. Read full

75222.subject: 2026 Is the Best Time to Build With AI. But the Moat Has Moved.
75222.body:
AI Frontier

Автор: Steve Nouri


Автор: Steve Nouri

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/2026-best-time-build-ai-moat-has-moved-steve-nouri-ixydc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B72LM4ZEEQBiuhnh2cfx7EA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0WARnAn8MXock1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqzd9cq5~xo-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqzd9cq5-xo
  
For years, the hard part was building. You needed engineers, capital, months of development, designers, writers, analysts, marketers, customer support, and operations just to get something decent into the market. AI changed that. A small team can now research faster, write faster, code faster, design faster, sell faster, support faster, and automate work that used to require entire departments. When building gets cheaper, more things get built. When more things get built, the market gets noisier. When the market gets noisier, the product alone is no longer enough. This is the shift most AI founders are still underestimating: The moat has moved. In the old software world, the moat was often the product. Better features. Better code. Better UX. Better integrations. Better engineering. That's why we spent the last 3 years building a distribution moat at GenAI.Works. Today, it's trusted by 200+ leading AI startups and enterprises, including NVIDIA, Oracle, Perplexity, Lovable, and many more. We're now welcoming you join us on this journey. Interest has already exceeded $2.8M, with just 2 days remaining. 1. Distribution Distribution is becoming the most important moat in AI. Not because product does not matter. Product matters more than ever. But product without distribution is invisible. And invisibility is getting more dangerous. Organic discovery is changing fast. Search is becoming answer-first. More people are getting answers directly from Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, and other AI systems. They do not always need to click. They do not always scroll. They do not always visit your website. The old game was simple: rank on Google, get the click, capture the lead, convert the customer. The new game is messier. Your content may be summarized before someone reaches you. Your website may be crawled more than it is visited. Your product may be mentioned by an AI answer, ignored by an AI answer, or replaced by an AI answer. Social media is also less predictable. Having followers does not mean your audience sees your posts. Platforms decide what gets shown. Algorithms change constantly. Organic reach gets harder. Paid acquisition gets more expensive. At the same time, AI makes it easier for everyone to create content. So the stage gets bigger. But it also gets louder. That is why distribution means everything. Distribution is not “

75221.subject: Teach Claude to work while you sleep.
75221.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/teach-claude-work-while-you-sleep-genai-works-51gue?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B2DdW2DclR%2FKkDvmw3urRpQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3WKCFh4j84ock1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqzaqnbk~3c-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqzaqnbk-3c
  
Welcome back! This edition runs from the practical to the foundational. You will learn to put your own weekly tasks on autopilot, then watch the biggest labs do the same thing one layer down, from custom silicon to AI that trains the next AI. The thread tying it together is ownership: who controls the stack, from the chip to the data to the documents your agents read.  In today's Atlas Newsletter: Build your first Claude automation in 3 steps. A plain-language ladder from teaching Claude one weekly task to having it run in the cloud while you sleep. OpenAI just shipped its own silicon. Jalapeño, OpenAI's first chip, built with Broadcom in a record nine-month tape-out and aimed squarely at LLM inference. Meta built an AI that teaches other AIs. Autodata turns an agent into a data scientist that designs and refines its own training data, with double-digit gains on hard subjects. RAG rediscovers your docs every time. OpenKB compiles them once. A vectorless, Obsidian-readable knowledge base that builds your documents into an inspectable wiki instead of re-deriving them per query. PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. Build your first Claude automation in 3 steps  You can set up your first Claude automation today. Think of it as a 3-step ladder. You start by teaching Claude one task you do every week, and you finish with that task running on its own while you sleep. Level 1: Teach Claude the task (in Claude chat) Start with something you find yourself redoing every week. Show Claude how you do it, once, in a normal chat. Then save those steps as a "skill," which is just a saved set of instructions Claude can reuse. After that, one keyword brings it back anytime, so you never explain it again. Try a prompt like this: "Create a skill that pulls [the input] from [tool], does [the task], and writes the result back to [where it lives]" Level 2: Put it on a schedule (in Cowork) Once the skill exists, you can tell Cowork to run it for you automatically. Say which skill to run and when, answer a couple of quick questions it asks to fill in the details, and that is it. The task now runs on a timer. You reuse the exact same skill from Level 1, so there is nothing to rebuild. Try a prompt like this: "Run my [skill name] skill every Monday at 9am and leave the outpu

75218.subject: Speed Has Value
75218.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/eaa70932-7ce4-467e-b338-a662ed575d6a/0xR_NL_Banner_Heading_Wordmark.jpg?t=1776775276)
Caption: 

Happy Monday! 

BTC led the major benchmarks lower as ETF outflows and Strategy-related pressure weighed on sentiment. At the same time, lending moved in the opposite direction, with Aave standing out as the market started to price its role in tokenized securities, institutional collateral markets, and onchain securities finance.

That same theme, crypto protocols monetizing more specific forms of financial utility, shows up in Hyperliquid as well. Its priority-fee market turns latency competition into a protocol-level revenue stream, allowing HyperCore to capture some of the value that previously accrued mainly to the fastest market makers and HFTs.

———————————————————————————

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/42e942ec-f31d-4957-8722-a3ac7b121e1e/0xR_NL_Market_Update.png?t=1767631151) [Market Update]
Caption: 

Markets slipped back into risk-off mode last week, with all four major benchmarks ending lower. The Nasdaq and S&P 500 fell -3.20% and -1.31%, respectively, while gold declined 3.31%. BTC was the weakest performer, dropping -6.74% on the week after briefly touching $58K. The asset is now down -31.9% YTD, making it the worst-performing major benchmark by a wide margin.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/057066f2-804c-4903-a6bc-91ca1ab7da17/image.png?t=1782734998)
Caption: 

The weakness in equities was once again centered around the AI trade. While Micron briefly lifted sentiment after reporting strong earnings and guiding for $50B of revenue next quarter, well [ahead](https://www.straitstimes.com/business/sia-shares-jump-amid-iran-peace-talks-world-cup-fever-but-rebound-could-be-temporary) of the $43.6B consensus, optimism faded after Apple announced price increases across its product lineup to offset higher chip costs. The move reignited concerns that rising AI infrastructure costs could eventually weigh on consumer demand while also adding to broader inflation pressures. 

BTC also faced several crypto-specific headwinds. Michael Saylor's STRC preferred shares fell 16.5% last week and [now trade](https://app.hyperliquid.xyz/trade/xyz:STRC) at roughly $76, well below their $100 par value. The discount reflects growing skepticism over the sustainability of STRC's dividend and has fueled speculation that additional BTC sales may be required to support the structure. At the same time, spot BTC ETFs recorded another $1.45B of net outflows, continuing the persistent institutional selling pressure seen over recent weeks.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect
